_note: Manually maintained grants. Status is auto-recomputed from 'dl' on each scraper run. For rolling grants set 'rolling': true. Add new grants here; they merge with scraped ICMR + ANRF data.

grants.title: MRC Research Grant – Applicant-Led (Rolling)
grants.focus: Causes & mechanisms of disease; diagnosis, treatment & prevention. Clinical pharmacology, drug safety, pharmacovigilance, cardiac pharmacotherapy all in MRC remit.
elig: Must be employed by eligible UK research organisation (UK lead required)
International co-investigators allowed (not as lead PI)
First interviews: Nov–Dec 2026; decisions Dec 2026
inelig: Researchers outside UK as lead applicant
Uninvited resubmissions of applications submitted after Apr 1 2026

grants.title: MRC New Investigator Research Grant – Applicant-Led
grants.focus: First independent research for early-career UK health researchers. Pharmacotherapy, clinical pharmacy, drug safety all eligible under MRC remit.
elig: Early-career researchers at eligible UK institution
Must not have held independent PI-level grant >£400k
Health/biomedical focus within MRC remit
inelig: Senior PIs with prior large independent grants

grants.title: Global Infectious Disease Research Training (D43)
grants.focus: Infectious disease research training in LMICs. AMR pharmacotherapy, HIV/TB drug treatment, clinical trial capacity building. India fully eligible.
elig: Universities/research institutions in LMICs (India fully eligible)
US partner institution required
LOI due July 6, 2026 (30 days before application)

grants.title: Emerging Global Leader Award (K43)
grants.focus: Support early-career LMIC researchers toward independence. Clinical pharmacy, pharmacovigilance, drug safety, neurology pharmacotherapy all eligible.
elig: Early-career researchers based in LMICs (India eligible)
Confirmed US mentor and partner institution required
PhD or MD/equivalent required

grants.title: LAUNCHing Leaders for U.S. Global Health Research (D43)
grants.focus: Global health research leadership training in LMICs. Pharmacy researchers in global health, clinical trials, and implementation science eligible.
elig: Universities in LMICs (India eligible)
US partner institution required
Focus on training health research leaders

grants.title: Merck Research Grants 2026 – Open Innovation
grants.focus: 2026 focus: (1) In-vitro models for neuroinflammatory disease therapies – EUR 250k/yr (pharmacy-relevant). (2) AI in cell culture media & bioprocess development. (3) Condensate-based cell-free screening assays. Deep-dive workshop Nov/Dec 2026.
elig: Researchers globally, including India – academic and industry
Neuroinflammatory disease drug discovery directly eligible
First stage: non-confidential abstract only; shortlist invited to deep-dive workshop
inelig: Projects outside Merck's 2026 defined thematic areas

grants.title: Pfizer Independent Medical Education & Competitive Grants 2026
grants.focus: Medical education and disease-area research aligned with Pfizer therapeutic areas: cardiology, oncology, rare disease, anti-infectives, CNS. Clinical pharmacy education grants available.
elig: Non-profit academic, hospital and medical institutions globally (India eligible)
Must align with Pfizer's defined therapeutic priorities
Pharmacy schools and clinical research programmes eligible
inelig: Proposals outside Pfizer's therapeutic focus areas

grants.title: TDR Impact Grants – Infectious Diseases of Poverty
grants.dlLabel: Rolling cycles (check tdr.who.int for current open round)
grants.focus: Implementation research on infectious diseases of poverty in LMICs. Pharmacotherapy of NTDs, antibiotic stewardship, drug access, treatment adherence research.
elig: Researchers in LMICs (India – check cycle-specific regional eligibility)
Must address infectious diseases of poverty
Pharmacy researchers addressing drug access or adherence eligible

grants.title: 4th Call for Proposals – Pandemic Prevention, Preparedness & Response
grants.focus: Pandemic PPR in LMICs. Pharmaceutical preparedness, essential medicine stockpiling, vaccine logistics, AMR pandemic risk all eligible.
elig: Governments and eligible institutions in LMICs
Multi-country proposals strongly preferred
Health ministries and academic institutions as implementing partners
inelig: For-profit private entities as lead applicant

grants.title: Team Grant – GACD Implementation Science for NCDs
grants.focus: NCD implementation science: cardiovascular disease, diabetes, cancer, mental health in LMICs. Pharmacotherapy implementation, medication adherence, drug utilisation.
elig: Canadian PI required as lead
LMIC partnerships mandatory
Indian researchers welcome as co-investigators/LMIC partners

grants.title: India Alliance Fellowships – Phase III (Early/Intermediate/Senior)
grants.focus: Basic biomedical, clinical, and public health research. All career stages. Pharmacotherapy, drug safety, clinical trials, pharmacovigilance all in remit.
elig: Early Fellowship: ≤3 years post-PhD
Intermediate: ≤7 years post-PhD
Senior: established PIs with independent research track record
Indian national or based in India
inelig: Researchers holding another major fellowship simultaneously

grants.title: HFSP International Research Grants 2027 (Pre-Proposals ~Oct 2026)
grants.focus: Interdisciplinary life sciences research by international teams. Neuropharmacology, systems pharmacology, computational drug science eligible.
elig: International teams from min. 2 different countries
Indian PIs eligible as team members
Genuinely interdisciplinary projects required
inelig: Standard clinical projects without interdisciplinary novelty

grants.title: Indo-German Joint Research Projects – Social Health Sciences
grants.focus: Social determinants of health; interdisciplinary health-social research; public health policy. Health economics, pharmacoeconomics, health equity eligible.
elig: Indian social science/health researchers at ICSSR-affiliated institution
German co-PI from DFG-eligible institution required
Joint application from both sides required
inelig: Pure clinical science without social science component

grants.title: Indo-German Joint Medical Research (Next Call – Theme TBD)
grants.focus: Medical sciences joint research. Last theme: Toxicology (2022). New theme expected – pharmacovigilance, AMR, NCDs possible. Pharmacy/clinical research eligible.
grants.source: icmr.gov.in / dfg.de (next call anticipated – set alert)
elig: Indian researchers at ICMR-affiliated institutions
German co-PI at DFG-eligible institution required
Medical/health sciences focus; clinical pharmacy eligible

grants.title: Bio-AI Joint Call: High Performance Biomanufacturing (BioE3 Policy)
grants.focus: AI-driven biomanufacturing, drug synthesis, biosimilars, sustainable health product development. Drug delivery systems, biosimilar development, precision formulation directly eligible.
elig: Indian institutions, national research labs, pharma startups
Biotech/pharma companies as co-applicants welcome
Must align with BioE3 biomanufacturing policy areas
inelig: Foreign-only applicants without Indian institutional partner

grants.title: Grand Challenges Explorations – Fall 2026 Round (Anticipated)
grants.funding: USD 100,000 for 18 months (Phase I); up to USD 1M follow-on (Phase II)
grants.focus: High-risk, high-reward innovations in global health and development. Two-page application, no preliminary data required. Past topics: diagnostics, drug delivery, AMR, nutrition, infectious disease treatment. Pharmacy innovation, point-of-care drug monitoring, novel drug delivery all potentially eligible.
grants.source: gcgh.grandchallenges.org (Spring 2026 closed Apr 28; Fall 2026 anticipated – sign up for alerts)
elig: Researchers globally including India — all countries eligible
Individuals, institutions, startups, and NGOs all eligible
Two-page application only — no preliminary data required
Grand Challenges India (DBT + BIRAC + Gates + Wellcome) also available separately for Indian applicants
Fall round typically opens Sep–Oct each year
inelig: Incremental or conventional research approaches (must be high-risk/high-reward)
Projects outside Gates Foundation disease and development priorities

grants.funder: Grand Challenges India (DBT / BIRAC / Gates / Wellcome)
grants.title: Grand Challenges India – Next Round (Anticipated 2026)
grants.dlLabel: Next India round TBD – monitor birac.nic.in & gcgh.grandchallenges.org
grants.funding: USD 100,000 (Phase I); up to USD 1M (Phase II)
grants.focus: Indian-specific Grand Challenges rounds co-funded by DBT, BIRAC, Gates Foundation, and Wellcome Trust. Innovations in health, diagnostics, drug delivery, infectious disease, nutrition. Indian pharmacy researchers directly eligible.
grants.source: birac.nic.in / gcgh.grandchallenges.org (Grand Challenges India – monitor both portals)
elig: Indian researchers at recognised institutions (India-specific round)
Individuals, startups, academic institutions all eligible
Two-page application, no preliminary data required
Must address health innovation challenges relevant to India/LMICs
Pharmacy innovation, affordable diagnostics, drug delivery directly eligible
inelig: Projects outside Gates Foundation / DBT health innovation priorities
Incremental research (must propose genuinely novel approach)

grants.title: EDCTP3 2026 Open Calls – TB, HIV, LRTIs, Pharmacovigilance & Climate/Health
grants.focus: 2026 topics: (1) TB drug development for adults & children – Africa. (2) Prevention & treatment of lower respiratory tract infections (LRTIs). (3) HIV co-infections & co-morbidities management. (4) Climate & health in sub-Saharan Africa. (5) Ethics, regulatory & PHARMACOVIGILANCE training networks (CSA – directly relevant). (6) Digital innovation & AI in integrated healthcare. Stage 1 closed Mar 4; full proposals Sep 17.
grants.source: global-health-edctp3.europa.eu (verified: full proposals Sep 17, 2026; EUR 147M pool)
elig: Research consortia with mandatory African partner institution
European/Horizon-associated country institutions as lead
PHARMACOVIGILANCE training networks (CSA topic 5) — directly relevant to JSS pharmacy
Regulatory science, ethics, and drug safety capacity building eligible
Must involve clinical trial or implementation study in sub-Saharan Africa
inelig: Stage 1 deadline (Mar 4, 2026) has passed — only full proposals invited now

grants.funder: ADB – NOTE: Not Applicable for Academic Researchers
grants.title: ⚠ Asian Development Bank (ADB) – Sovereign Grants Only (Not for Researchers)
grants.dlLabel: N/A – ADB grants go to governments, not academic researchers
grants.focus: ADB provides grants to governments of developing member countries, not directly to academic researchers or universities. The government manages ADB grants and issues sub-calls for implementing firms. Indian pharmacy researchers CANNOT apply directly to ADB. If interested in ADB-funded health projects, contact India's Ministry of Health or state health departments that receive ADB financing.
grants.source: adb.org (verified – sovereign grants only, not open to direct academic applications)
elig: Government ministries and sovereign bodies of ADB developing member countries only
Academic institutions may be sub-contracted by governments for specific ADB-funded project components
inelig: Universities and research institutions as lead applicants
JSS College of Pharmacy cannot apply directly to ADB
